title: Break-Glass â€” Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:breakglass" earliest=-30d
| eval has_reason=if(isnotnull(reason_code) AND reason_code!="","OK","MISSING")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d short_description="*break*glass*"
    | rename number as attestation_ticket, sys_id as session_id ]
| eval reviewed=if(isnotnull(attestation_ticket),"Yes","No")
| where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed
description: Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.
value: Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.
implementation: (1) Map `session_id` join keys to your ticketing design; (2) 24h alert on missing reason; (3) 72h alert on missing attestation; (4) quarterly privacy committee rollup.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
â€˘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:breakglass` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `reason_code`, `session_id`), `index=itsm` ServiceNow tasks (`short_description`, `parent_incident`).
â€˘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
(1) Map `session_id` join keys to your ticketing design; (2) 24h alert on missing reason; (3) 72h alert on missing attestation; (4) quarterly privacy committee rollup.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:breakglass" earliest=-30d
| eval has_reason=if(isnotnull(reason_code) AND reason_code!="","OK","MISSING")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d short_description="*break*glass*"
    | rename number as attestation_ticket, sys_id as session_id ]
| eval reviewed=if(isnotnull(attestation_ticket),"Yes","No")
| where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed
```

Understanding this SPL

**Break-Glass â€” Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review (Â§164.502(a)(2)(ii) / policy)** â€” Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:breakglass` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `reason_code`, `session_id`), `index=itsm` ServiceNow tasks (`short_description`, `parent_incident`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:breakglass. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:breakglass", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€˘ `eval` defines or adjusts **has_reason** â€” often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
â€˘ Joins to a subsearch with `join` â€” set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
â€˘ `eval` defines or adjusts **reviewed** â€” often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
â€˘ Filters the current rows with `where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"` â€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
â€˘ Pipeline stage (see **Break-Glass â€” Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review (Â§164.502(a)(2)(ii) / policy)**): table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed


Step 3 â€” Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (open attestations), Table (violations), Single value (% reviewed).
